name: Sauteed Cubed Veal in Cream Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a small mixing bowl with a sieve large enough to hold the meat.
2. Cut the veal into 1/2-inch cubes.
3. Sprinkle the veal with salt and pepper.
4. Heat half the oil in a heavy skillet until very hot and just starting to smoke.
5. Add the veal to the skillet without crowding it. Cook over high heat, stirring occasionally, until the meat is lightly browned, about 1 1/2 minutes. Transfer the cooked meat to the sieve.
6. Add the remaining oil to the skillet and heat until very hot and just starting to smoke. Add the remaining veal without overcrowding the pan. Cook over high heat, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned. Transfer the cooked meat to the sieve.
7. Pour out the oil from the bowl.
8. Wipe the skillet clean.
9. Add half of the butter to the skillet and let it melt. Add the shallots and cook briefly, stirring, until they are translucent.
10. Add the white wine and cook until it has reduced by about half or slightly more.
11. Add the heavy cream and any accumulated liquid from the meat. Cook for a few minutes over high heat until thickened.
12. Return the cooked veal to the skillet with the sauce. Turn to coat the meat in the sauce, then reheat briefly. Stir in the remaining butter. Sprinkle with parsley, tarragon, and chives.
French name: Emince` de veau a` la suisse
An ideal accompaniment for this dish is spätzle or Swiss-style dumplings.
